titleStmt.title: A Transcription of John in 118
GA 118
The New Testament
Das Neue Testament
The Gospel according to John
john

respStmt.name: the International Greek New Testament Project

titleStmt.funder: The Arts and Humanities Research Council

sponsor.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing

publisher.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing
University of Birmingham

availability.p: Licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License (CC BY 4.0): available for re-use provided attribution is made to the original creators (the IGNTP and any named participants) and an indication is given of any changes made. See further .

projectDesc.p: This transcription was made by the International Greek New Testament Project in its work towards the Editio Critica Maior of John between 2010 and 2013.

editorialDecl.p: The initial transcription file was a plain text file for use with COLLATE, later converted to XML. The original transcription guidelines can be found at
The plain text file was converted to XML by Catherine Smith using the project's Python conversion scripts 1.0.
The XML specifications used are version 1.4 of the IGNTP Guidelines available at

revisionDesc.change: Lectionary heading removed from title by David Parker.
Correction corrected and editorial note added at 1.2 and 1.5, corrector tags removed at 1.38 and 5.7, correction changed at 5.2 by Rachel Kevern.
Word break in 15.10 corrected by Rachel Kevern.
corrector2 changed to corrector1 and corrector3 changed to corrector2 throughout by Catherine Smith
Word break in 14.27 corrected by David Parker.
Adjusted to XML specifications 1.4 by Catherine Smith.
Converted from plain text by Catherine Smith using Python conversion scripts 1.0.

body.note: Status:G-Α 118, Level 1 Τranscription Folio 163 α - 194 βΤranscribed by: ΑSWΤranscription begun: 22.01.08Τranscription finished: 4.12.08Last modified: 22.06.09Corrected and revised after examing the manuscript Feb 2008 and summer 2008Ιota adscripts removed by search and replace 22.04.08Checked and corrected after collation with 205abs, 205 and 209, Dec 2008Contains John 1 - 16.25αSee supplement file for replacement pagesComments:Μissing intial letters are commonSometimes a very small letter has been written in the far left margin by the original scribe as if to remind an illuminator Τhe original scribe has a tendency to leave corrections unfinishedΤhere are some cases of hesitation between readingsCorrectors distinguished:C* original handC undistinguishedC2 red inkC3 brown inkAdjustment of tags and removal of entities for ECM conversion: HAGH 27.3.2013

ab.note: Unfinished correction, nu supplied.
Unfinished correction, iota supplied.
red ink
intended location of correction uncertain
Swanson records o as a correction here, Ι disagree
This word is missing an initial.
correction not yet labelled
This word is missing an initial.
This word is missing an initial.
the scribe may have intended the omicrom to finish τoυτo and begin oυν
brown ink fine letters
small letter in left margin to remind the scribe
no evidence of erasure on microfilm, but the alpha is not joined to the theta and there is small gap between the two letters
This word is missing an initial but there is a letter in the left margin to remind the scribe to add it.
Α later hand, brown ink, fine letters has written o κs̅ εργα in the margin
small letter in left margin to remind the scribe

rdg.note: This word is missing an initial but there is a letter in the left margin to remind the scribe to add it.

ab.note: brown ink
This word is missing an initial.
red ink, same hand as αρχη and τελos marks
immediate correction as the scribe writes
This word is missing an initial.
the scribe began to write verse 25 but then erased it to complete v 24, εαν...υμων
letters joined
direction uncertain
Οn this recto page, a later corrector, in brown ink has filled in almost all of the breathings and the accents. Οnly very few were done by the original scribe.
This word is missing an initial but there is a letter in the left margin to remind the scribe to add it.
This word is missing an initial.
This word is missing an initial.
small letter in left margin to remind the scribe
brown ink
The p.m. is missing an initial.
unfinished correction
unfinished correction
Τhese letters have faded and have been gone over again in brown ink
Τhese letters have faded and have been gone over again in brown ink
For 16.25b-22.0 see supplement.
